stemHtml: Which statement correctly describes the operation of the automatic flight control system (AFCS) mixed modes

options.a: In the mixed mode for the vertical path, the autopilot uses a pitch attitude (or pitch attitude rate) which allows it to avoid a tail-strike during take off, and for the lateral path, it uses the localizer signal to keep the runway centreline.
options.b: The mixed mode commands pitch to maintain optimum climb speed and uses a heading/track mode to keep the centreline of the runway.
options.c: It works on the same principle as the CWS and reduces the pilot’s workload for flight path management.
options.d: The mixed modes are exclusively used for the low visibility take-off and landing.
